{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "CRM Administrator",
  "description": "Quadri Advisory HR Services Limited is a dynamic Human Resource and Business Advisory firm committed to delivering innovative workforce solutions and strategic consulting services to organizations across various industries. &lrm;We specialize in helping businesses attract, develop, manage, and retain top talent while improving operational efficiency and organizational performance.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: CRM Administrator

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Description


	We&#39;re looking for a highly organized and collaborative CRM Administrator to join our Revenue Operations Team.
	As a CRM Administrator, you will manage and enhance our CRM system&#39;s functionality, work closely with various team members to understand business needs, and implement solutions.
	Your role is vital in maintaining system integrity, enhancing user experience, and ensuring the delivery of key insights to drive business decisions.
	If you thrive in a fast-paced environment and are passionate about improving operations through technology, we&#39;d love to hear from you.


Responsibilities


	Understand the commercial operation, challenges, and objectives of each team; improve their operation through CRM administration and consultation.
	Provide system administration support for the CRM, focusing on user permissions, custom objects, workflows, and profiles.
	Create, manage, and optimize complex workflow rules, data validation, and approval processes.
	Create and manage custom objects, fields, formulas, record types, page layouts, reports, and dashboards.
	Analyze data to provide insights into team operation, effectiveness, and productivity.
	Work closely with other team members to capture business requirements and implement CRM solutions.
	Coordinate and support integrations with third-party apps, plugins, and tools.
	Support daily troubleshooting, bug fixing, user adoption, training, and documentation.
	Identify and resolve data anomalies, maintaining high data integrity.


Requirements


	2+ years of CRM Administration experience (e.g., Salesforce, Microsoft Dynamics).
	Experience working directly with sales teams and implementing their requirements in the CRM system.
	Ability to administer and configure a CRM system (custom objects, workflows, permissions, etc.).
	Familiarity with automating business processes, creating reports/dashboards, and data analysis/modeling.
	Strong communication, collaboration, and problem-solving skills.
	Bonus points for Salesforce.com certification, experience.


Salary
N300,000 - N350,000 per month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified&nbsp;candidates should send their&nbsp;CV to:&nbsp;quadolaadvisoryhrservicesltd@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

  "validThrough": "2026-05-14",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Quadri Advisory HR Services Limited"
    
  },

    "@type": "Place",

      "@type": "PostalAddress",
       "postalCode": ". ",

       "streetAddress": ". ",
      "addressLocality": "Lagos",
      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }
  },

    "name": "",
    "value": "198043"
  },

    "@type": "MonetaryAmount",
    "currency": "NGN",
    "value": {

      "minValue": 250000,

    }

CRM Administrator Job at Quadri Advisory HR Services Limited




        
    

        
        Quadri Advisory HR Services Limited is a dynamic Human Resource and Business Advisory firm committed to delivering innovative workforce solutions and strategic consulting services to organizations across various industries. ‎We specialize in helping businesses attract, develop, manage, and retain top talent while improving operational efficiency and organizational performance.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: CRM Administrator

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We're looking for a highly organized and collaborative CRM Administrator to join our Revenue Operations Team.
	As a CRM Administrator, you will manage and enhance our CRM system's functionality, work closely with various team members to understand business needs, and implement solutions.
	Your role is vital in maintaining system integrity, enhancing user experience, and ensuring the delivery of key insights to drive business decisions.
	If you thrive in a fast-paced environment and are passionate about improving operations through technology, we'd love to hear from you.


Responsibilities


	Understand the commercial operation, challenges, and objectives of each team; improve their operation through CRM administration and consultation.
	Provide system administration support for the CRM, focusing on user permissions, custom objects, workflows, and profiles.
	Create, manage, and optimize complex workflow rules, data validation, and approval processes.
	Create and manage custom objects, fields, formulas, record types, page layouts, reports, and dashboards.
	Analyze data to provide insights into team operation, effectiveness, and productivity.
	Work closely with other team members to capture business requirements and implement CRM solutions.
	Coordinate and support integrations with third-party apps, plugins, and tools.
	Support daily troubleshooting, bug fixing, user adoption, training, and documentation.
	Identify and resolve data anomalies, maintaining high data integrity.


Requirements


	2+ years of CRM Administration experience (e.g., Salesforce, Microsoft Dynamics).
	Experience working directly with sales teams and implementing their requirements in the CRM system.
	Ability to administer and configure a CRM system (custom objects, workflows, permissions, etc.).
	Familiarity with automating business processes, creating reports/dashboards, and data analysis/modeling.
	Strong communication, collaboration, and problem-solving skills.
	Bonus points for Salesforce.com certification, experience.


Salary
N300,000 - N350,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "description": "Henosis International is a youth led nonprofit organization passionate about spreading love, support and empowerment through meaningful projects.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Graphic Designer (Volunteer)
Job Location:&nbsp;Ibadan, Oyo&nbsp;

Responsibilities


	Design graphics for events, campaigns, and social media
	Create visually engaging materials that align with our brand
	Work with the publicity team to support campaigns
	Translate ideas into compelling visual content


Who We Are Looking For:


	&nbsp;Skilled in tools like Canva, Photoshop, or similar
	&nbsp;Creative thinkers with attention to detail
	&nbsp;Individuals passionate about impactdriven work
	&nbsp;Ability to meet deadlines and collaborate effectively.


Method of Application&nbsp;

Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;faith@henosisinternational.orgusing Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Graphic Designer (Volunteer) Job at Henosis International




        
    

        
        Henosis International is a youth led nonprofit organization passionate about spreading love, support and empowerment through meaningful projects.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Graphic Designer (Volunteer)
Job Location: Ibadan, Oyo 

Responsibilities


	Design graphics for events, campaigns, and social media
	Create visually engaging materials that align with our brand
	Work with the publicity team to support campaigns
	Translate ideas into compelling visual content


Who We Are Looking For:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	 Skilled in tools like Canva, Photoshop, or similar
	 Creative thinkers with attention to detail
	 Individuals passionate about impactdriven work
	 Ability to meet deadlines and collaborate effectively.


Method of Application 

Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected]using Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Secretary",
  "description": "MISHRA Global Resources Limited -&nbsp;We are a human capital company that creates value and invest in people development and growth. The aim is to see this translate to personal wellbeing, productivity, and efficiencynin businesses, workplaces and the community.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Secretary

Job Location:&nbsp;Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Working days: Monday -Saturday

About the Role


	As the Company Secretary, you will be the first point of contact for our walk-in clients, corporate partners, and service technicians.
	You will manage the daily administrative operations of the office, ensure inquiries are handled professionally, and provide vital support to the sales and technical teams so they can focus on closing deals and completing installations.


Core Responsibilities


	Front Desk Management: Greet and assist walk-in clients, vendors, and partners with a warm and professional demeanor.
	Customer Communication: Manage incoming phone calls, emails, and social media inquiries. Provide basic product information and route technical or sales queries to the appropriate departments.
	Administrative Support: Draft letters, memos, and invoices. Maintain an organized filing system for client contracts, warranties, and service schedules.
	Sales &amp; Service Coordination: Assist the sales team in logging new leads and help the technical team schedule installation and maintenance appointments.
	Office Operations: Ensure the reception and general office areas are clean, professional, and well-stocked with necessary office supplies.


Requirements &amp; Qualifications


	Education: Minimum of OND or BSc Degree in Business Administration, Secretarial Studies, or a related field.
	Experience: 1&ndash;3 years of proven experience as a Secretary, Front Desk Executive, or Administrative Assistant. (Experience in the energy, tech, or sales sector is a strong advantage).
	Tech-Savvy: Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) and comfortable using email and basic CRM tools.
	Communication Skills: Excellent spoken and written English. You must be able to communicate clearly and politely with a diverse range of clients.
	Organization: Strong multitasking skills with an eye for detail.
	Proximity: Due to the 6-day work week and working hours, candidates residing in or very close to Ikeja are strongly preferred.


What We Offer


	Salary: N100,000 - N120,000 monthly.
	Stability: A secure role in one of the fastest-growing industries in Nigeria.
	Growth: Opportunity to learn about the lucrative renewable energy market and grow alongside a company dedicated to empowering its people.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their updated CV and a brief introductory email to:&nbsp;business@mishraglobal.com&nbsp;using &quot;New Secretary Ikeja&rdquo; as the subject of the email.",

Secretary Job at MISHRA Global Resources Limited




        
    

        
        MISHRA Global Resources Limited - We are a human capital company that creates value and invest in people development and growth. The aim is to see this translate to personal wellbeing, productivity, and efficiencynin businesses, workplaces and the community.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Secretary

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Working days: Monday -Saturday

About the Role
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	As the Company Secretary, you will be the first point of contact for our walk-in clients, corporate partners, and service technicians.
	You will manage the daily administrative operations of the office, ensure inquiries are handled professionally, and provide vital support to the sales and technical teams so they can focus on closing deals and completing installations.


Core Responsibilities


	Front Desk Management: Greet and assist walk-in clients, vendors, and partners with a warm and professional demeanor.
	Customer Communication: Manage incoming phone calls, emails, and social media inquiries. Provide basic product information and route technical or sales queries to the appropriate departments.
	Administrative Support: Draft letters, memos, and invoices. Maintain an organized filing system for client contracts, warranties, and service schedules.
	Sales & Service Coordination: Assist the sales team in logging new leads and help the technical team schedule installation and maintenance appointments.
	Office Operations: Ensure the reception and general office areas are clean, professional, and well-stocked with necessary office supplies.


Requirements & Qualifications


	Education: Minimum of OND or BSc Degree in Business Administration, Secretarial Studies, or a related field.
	Experience: 1–3 years of proven experience as a Secretary, Front Desk Executive, or Administrative Assistant. (Experience in the energy, tech, or sales sector is a strong advantage).
	Tech-Savvy: Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) and comfortable using email and basic CRM tools.
	Communication Skills: Excellent spoken and written English. You must be able to communicate clearly and politely with a diverse range of clients.
	Organization: Strong multitasking skills with an eye for detail.
	Proximity: Due to the 6-day work week and working hours, candidates residing in or very close to Ikeja are strongly preferred.


What We Offer


	Salary: N100,000 - N120,000 monthly.
	Stability: A secure role in one of the fastest-growing industries in Nigeria.
	Growth: Opportunity to learn about the lucrative renewable energy market and grow alongside a company dedicated to empowering its people.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their updated CV and a brief introductory email to: [email protected] using "New Secretary Ikeja” as the subject of the email.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Business Analyst",
  "description": "First Bank of Nigeria Limited (FirstBank) is Nigeria&#39;s largest financial services institution by total assets and gross earnings. With more than 10 million customer accounts, FirstBank has over 750 branches providing a comprehensive range of retail and corporate financial services. The Bank has international presence through its subsidiaries, FBN Bank (UK) Limited in London and Paris, FBNBank DRC, FBNBank Ghana, FBNBank Gambia, FBNBank Guinea, FBNBank Sierra-Leone and FBNBank Senegal, as well as its Representative Offices in Johannesburg, Beijing and Abu Dhabi.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Business Analyst

Job Identification:&nbsp;1725
Job Location:&nbsp;Nigeria
Job Schedule: Full time

Duties &amp; Responsibilties


	Support the Finance Business Partner in budgeting, forecasting, and financial planning for various business units.
	Conduct detailed financial analysis, variance analysis, and trend reporting to inform business performance and identify improvement opportunities.
	Assist in the preparation and review of monthly, quarterly, and annual management reports for senior management and regulatory bodies.
	Collaborate with business units to gather, validate, and document financial and operational data, ensuring accuracy and completeness.
	Identify, recommend, and track key performance indicators (KPIs) in line with business objectives and regulatory requirements.
	Develop business cases and financial models to support new initiatives, product launches, or strategic projects.
	Support in facilitating financial review meetings, preparing presentations and supporting documentation as needed.
	Ensure compliance with internal controls, financial policies, and regulatory standards applicable to the Nigerian banking sector.
	Act as a liaison between finance, IT, risk, and other support departments to resolve issues and drive process improvements.
	Participate in special assignments and ad hoc projects as directed by the Finance Business Partner and Head of Finance.
	Identify and collate appropriate data for scorecard generation&nbsp;
	Cascade the business unit&rsquo;s budget to the the groups, teams, desks and relationship managers
	Prepare customer profitability analysis for RMs on request.
	Track performance effectively.
	Generate and communicate scorecards to End-Users
	Assist in the resolution of conflicts arising from Scorecards, MIS, Expense control, Budget of these groups
	Facilitate robust performance dialogues.
	Facilitate enlightenment/training sessions on scorecards and other Performance Initiatives.
	Maintain the Security of all Information entrusted to the staff.
	Any other responsibilities as may be assigned by Business Partner


Job Requirements
Education:


	First Degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics, Science or Engineering.
	Master&rsquo;s Degree/Professional certification in Accounting or Finance (ACA, CIBN or similar professional qualifications).


Experience:


	Experience in MIS (Management Information System).
	Minimum of 6 years of relevant experience, in MIS, Finance, Forecasting, budgeting or finance role
	Proficiency in Microsoft office suite and leading analytics tools, SQL, etc.
	Experience in Finance or Accounting.

Business Analyst Job at First Bank of Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        First Bank of Nigeria Limited (FirstBank) is Nigeria's largest financial services institution by total assets and gross earnings. With more than 10 million customer accounts, FirstBank has over 750 branches providing a comprehensive range of retail and corporate financial services. The Bank has international presence through its subsidiaries, FBN Bank (UK) Limited in London and Paris, FBNBank DRC, FBNBank Ghana, FBNBank Gambia, FBNBank Guinea, FBNBank Sierra-Leone and FBNBank Senegal, as well as its Representative Offices in Johannesburg, Beijing and Abu Dhabi.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Analyst
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Identification: 1725
Job Location: Nigeria
Job Schedule: Full time

Duties & Responsibilties


	Support the Finance Business Partner in budgeting, forecasting, and financial planning for various business units.
	Conduct detailed financial analysis, variance analysis, and trend reporting to inform business performance and identify improvement opportunities.
	Assist in the preparation and review of monthly, quarterly, and annual management reports for senior management and regulatory bodies.
	Collaborate with business units to gather, validate, and document financial and operational data, ensuring accuracy and completeness.
	Identify, recommend, and track key performance indicators (KPIs) in line with business objectives and regulatory requirements.
	Develop business cases and financial models to support new initiatives, product launches, or strategic projects.
	Support in facilitating financial review meetings, preparing presentations and supporting documentation as needed.
	Ensure compliance with internal controls, financial policies, and regulatory standards applicable to the Nigerian banking sector.
	Act as a liaison between finance, IT, risk, and other support departments to resolve issues and drive process improvements.
	Participate in special assignments and ad hoc projects as directed by the Finance Business Partner and Head of Finance.
	Identify and collate appropriate data for scorecard generation 
	Cascade the business unit’s budget to the the groups, teams, desks and relationship managers
	Prepare customer profitability analysis for RMs on request.
	Track performance effectively.
	Generate and communicate scorecards to End-Users
	Assist in the resolution of conflicts arising from Scorecards, MIS, Expense control, Budget of these groups
	Facilitate robust performance dialogues.
	Facilitate enlightenment/training sessions on scorecards and other Performance Initiatives.
	Maintain the Security of all Information entrusted to the staff.
	Any other responsibilities as may be assigned by Business Partner


Job Requirements
Education:


	First Degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics, Science or Engineering.
	Master’s Degree/Professional certification in Accounting or Finance (ACA, CIBN or similar professional qualifications).


Experience:


	Experience in MIS (Management Information System).
	Minimum of 6 years of relevant experience, in MIS, Finance, Forecasting, budgeting or finance role
	Proficiency in Microsoft office suite and leading analytics tools, SQL, etc.
	Experience in Finance or Accounting.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

Duties &amp; Responsibilties


	Support the Finance Business Partner in budgeting, forecasting, and financial planning for various business units.
	Conduct detailed financial analysis, variance analysis, and trend reporting to inform business performance and identify improvement opportunities.
	Assist in the preparation and review of monthly, quarterly, and annual management reports for senior management and regulatory bodies.
	Collaborate with business units to gather, validate, and document financial and operational data, ensuring accuracy and completeness.
	Identify, recommend, and track key performance indicators (KPIs) in line with business objectives and regulatory requirements.
	Develop business cases and financial models to support new initiatives, product launches, or strategic projects.
	Support in facilitating financial review meetings, preparing presentations and supporting documentation as needed.
	Ensure compliance with internal controls, financial policies, and regulatory standards applicable to the Nigerian banking sector.
	Act as a liaison between finance, IT, risk, and other support departments to resolve issues and drive process improvements.
	Participate in special assignments and ad hoc projects as directed by the Finance Business Partner and Head of Finance.
	Identify and collate appropriate data for scorecard generation&nbsp;
	Cascade the business unit&rsquo;s budget to the the groups, teams, desks and relationship managers
	Prepare customer profitability analysis for RMs on request.
	Track performance effectively.
	Generate and communicate scorecards to End-Users
	Assist in the resolution of conflicts arising from Scorecards, MIS, Expense control, Budget of these groups
	Facilitate robust performance dialogues.
	Facilitate enlightenment/training sessions on scorecards and other Performance Initiatives.
	Maintain the Security of all Information entrusted to the staff.
	Any other responsibilities as may be assigned by Business Partner


Job Requirements
Education:


	First Degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics, Science or Engineering.
	Master&rsquo;s Degree/Professional certification in Accounting or Finance (ACA, CIBN or similar professional qualifications).


Experience:


	Experience in MIS (Management Information System).
	Minimum of 6 years of relevant experience, in MIS, Finance, Forecasting, budgeting or finance role
	Proficiency in Microsoft office suite and leading analytics tools, SQL, etc.
	Experience in Finance or Accounting.

Business Analyst Job at First Bank of Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        First Bank of Nigeria Limited (FirstBank) is Nigeria's largest financial services institution by total assets and gross earnings. With more than 10 million customer accounts, FirstBank has over 750 branches providing a comprehensive range of retail and corporate financial services. The Bank has international presence through its subsidiaries, FBN Bank (UK) Limited in London and Paris, FBNBank DRC, FBNBank Ghana, FBNBank Gambia, FBNBank Guinea, FBNBank Sierra-Leone and FBNBank Senegal, as well as its Representative Offices in Johannesburg, Beijing and Abu Dhabi.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Analyst
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Identification: 1725
Job Location: Nigeria
Job Schedule: Full time

Duties & Responsibilties


	Support the Finance Business Partner in budgeting, forecasting, and financial planning for various business units.
	Conduct detailed financial analysis, variance analysis, and trend reporting to inform business performance and identify improvement opportunities.
	Assist in the preparation and review of monthly, quarterly, and annual management reports for senior management and regulatory bodies.
	Collaborate with business units to gather, validate, and document financial and operational data, ensuring accuracy and completeness.
	Identify, recommend, and track key performance indicators (KPIs) in line with business objectives and regulatory requirements.
	Develop business cases and financial models to support new initiatives, product launches, or strategic projects.
	Support in facilitating financial review meetings, preparing presentations and supporting documentation as needed.
	Ensure compliance with internal controls, financial policies, and regulatory standards applicable to the Nigerian banking sector.
	Act as a liaison between finance, IT, risk, and other support departments to resolve issues and drive process improvements.
	Participate in special assignments and ad hoc projects as directed by the Finance Business Partner and Head of Finance.
	Identify and collate appropriate data for scorecard generation 
	Cascade the business unit’s budget to the the groups, teams, desks and relationship managers
	Prepare customer profitability analysis for RMs on request.
	Track performance effectively.
	Generate and communicate scorecards to End-Users
	Assist in the resolution of conflicts arising from Scorecards, MIS, Expense control, Budget of these groups
	Facilitate robust performance dialogues.
	Facilitate enlightenment/training sessions on scorecards and other Performance Initiatives.
	Maintain the Security of all Information entrusted to the staff.
	Any other responsibilities as may be assigned by Business Partner


Job Requirements
Education:


	First Degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics, Science or Engineering.
	Master’s Degree/Professional certification in Accounting or Finance (ACA, CIBN or similar professional qualifications).


Experience:


	Experience in MIS (Management Information System).
	Minimum of 6 years of relevant experience, in MIS, Finance, Forecasting, budgeting or finance role
	Proficiency in Microsoft office suite and leading analytics tools, SQL, etc.
	Experience in Finance or Accounting.
